tickets.title: modify 対象 GitHub Issue 化 (scanner script + 運用文書 / side-effect free)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `scripts/scan-modify-targets.py` which scans the codebase for 4 categories of modify candidates (deprecated_deps / stale_routers / stale_services / archived_keyword) and outputs structured JSON to stdout or --out file. The scanner is read-only and does NOT create GitHub Issues directly; conversion to Issues is documented in `docs/modify-targets/README.md` via `gh` CLI.

When the scanner runs, it shall return a dict with keys `total` (int count), `by_category` (count per category), and `targets` (list of {category, file, line, snippet, reason}). Exit code 0 on success, 2 on invalid arguments.

While the scanner runs, the system shall NOT mutate repository state, shall NOT call any external API (no `gh` / `curl` / GitHub API), and shall NOT write to audit_logs DB. Output file is the only side effect (when --out is specified).

If --category is not in VALID_CATEGORIES, the system shall raise ValueError and exit with code 2. If --out path is unwritable, the system shall print error to stderr and exit with code 2. The scanner SHALL NOT contain hardcoded GitHub tokens or repo URLs (auth handled by external `gh` CLI in docs).

While the audit runs, the system shall not modify tickets.json (read-only) and shall not access external network.

Where a ticket already had its AC corrected in a prior PR (T-019-01 / T-S0-13 / T-001-01 / T-001-02 / T-001-04 / T-S0-08 / T-S0-09), the report shall mark it as 'previously_fixed' so reviewers can focus on remaining mismatches.

tickets.title: GitHub Actions ci.yml (pytest + coverage + pre-commit-check + smoke 統合)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CI workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yml` shall run on every pull_request to main and every push to main, executing 4 parallel jobs (pre-commit-check / backend-pytest / backend-smoke / summary) and shall fail the build (exit_code != 0) if any job fails. ci.yml reuses existing scripts/pre-commit-check.sh without re-implementing the structural lint logic (REUSE 精神, T-S0-03 と同じパターン).

When the workflow runs, it shall execute (1) bash scripts/pre-commit-check.sh --quick (構造 lint + backend smoke), (2) python3 -m pytest tests/ --cov with deselect for known numpy-compat failure, (3) python3 -c 'from main import app' import smoke, and shall upload coverage.xml as artifact with 7-day retention.

While a pull_request is open, the workflow status shall be visible in the GitHub UI and shall preserve pytest baseline (>= 2823 passing tests excluding deselect). The workflow shall NOT modify repository state (permissions: contents=read).

If pytest fails (excluding documented deselect), pre-commit-check fails, or main:app import fails, the workflow shall fail with exit_code != 0. continue-on-error: true shall NOT be used. The workflow shall NOT mutate persistent state (no writes, no audit_logs DB rows).

tickets.title: license-check.yml (AGPL 防御 + ADR-010 機械的ガード CI 実効化)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CI workflow `.github/workflows/license-check.yml` shall run on every pull_request to main and every push to main, and shall fail the build (exit_code != 0) if AGPL/LGPL licensed dependencies are detected or if ADR-010 §UNWANTED mechanical guards (LangGraph/LangChain in main runner path, emoji baseline drift, tickets baseline drift, ARCHIVE residual) are violated. The workflow re-uses existing scripts/lint-mock.sh and scripts/validate-tickets.py without re-implementing detection logic.

When the workflow runs, it shall execute in sequence: scripts/lint-mock.sh --agpl, --no-langgraph, --emoji, --secrets, --archive (5 invocations) and python3 scripts/validate-tickets.py (1 invocation), and shall record the exit code of each step in the GitHub Actions log.

While a pull_request is open, the workflow status shall be visible in the GitHub UI and shall preserve existing lint script baselines (emoji=0 / tickets=0 / no AGPL deps / no ARCHIVE residual / no LangGraph imports). The workflow shall NOT modify repository state.

If an AGPL/LGPL dep is introduced into backend/requirements.txt or frontend/package.json, the workflow shall fail with exit_code != 0. If LangGraph or LangChain imports appear in the main runner code path, the workflow shall fail (ADR-010 §UNWANTED). If validate-tickets.py reports issues > 0, the workflow shall fail. The workflow shall NOT mutate persistent state (no writes to repo, no audit_logs DB rows).

tickets.title: Supabase FE wrapper (singleton browser client + auth helpers + graceful degradation)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The frontend shall provide `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` exposing `getSupabaseClient() / getCurrentUser() / signOut() / isSupabaseConfigured() / SupabaseNotConfiguredError / SupabaseConfigError`. The wrapper shall coexist with existing `api.ts` (backend REST) without modifying it (REUSE). Supabase usage is opt-in for code paths that need Auth / Realtime / RLS-aware direct access.

When `getSupabaseClient()` is called, the system shall read NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY env vars, validate URL pattern (https://*.supabase.co), and return a singleton client (cached on first call). When `signOut()` is called, the session shall be cleared in browser storage.

While env vars are unset OR @supabase/supabase-js is not installed, the system shall return a mock client whose methods return { data: null, error: SupabaseNotConfiguredError } (no throw, build/tsc passes). While env vars are valid, the client shall persist sessions in localStorage (persistSession: true).

If NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL does not match `https://*.supabase.co/` pattern OR NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY length < 10, the system shall throw SupabaseConfigError. The module source SHALL NOT contain hardcoded URLs / anon keys / service keys (env-only). The wrapper SHALL NOT write to audit_logs DB (backend-only concern).

tickets.title: claude-agent-sdk runner 基盤 (subprocess + session resume)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The runner shall use claude-agent-sdk (Anthropic official) — NOT LangGraph — to spawn Claude Code as a subprocess and capture stdout/stderr.
The runner shall pass session_id to claude-agent-sdk so the SDK auto-restores conversation history and applies 3-tier compaction.
When a task starts, the system shall create a sessions row with status=running and stream logs to session_logs.
While the runner is active, the SDK shall apply prompt cache (cache_control: ephemeral, 5min TTL) automatically; the system shall record cache hit rate to cost_logs.
While context usage exceeds 95%, the SDK shall auto-generate a 9-section structured summary; the system shall persist it to chat_messages.compressed_summary.
When the subprocess crashes, the system shall mark status=crashed and offer 4-choice resume (from_checkpoint / rerun_full / manual_fix / cancel).
If LangGraph or LangChain imports are introduced anywhere in this module, the lint script shall fail (ADR-010 forbids them in main path).

tickets.title: OS-level sandbox 基盤 (bwrap on Linux / sandbox-exec on macOS)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall execute all subprocess commands invoked by claude-agent-sdk runner inside an OS-level sandbox: bwrap (bubblewrap) on Linux and sandbox-exec on macOS, configured via SandboxConfig (allow_paths, allow_hosts, read_only_paths).
When a sandboxed process attempts to write outside SandboxConfig.allow_paths, the OS layer shall block the syscall (bwrap --ro-bind for read-only roots, sandbox-exec deny for others).
While running in sandbox, the system shall deny outbound network by default (bwrap --unshare-net) and only open access to SandboxConfig.allow_hosts via an explicit relay or empty by default.
Where SandboxConfig.allow_hosts is empty, run_sandboxed shall NOT enable network at all (zero-trust default for runner subprocesses).

If a sandboxed process exits with a sandbox-violation signal (SIGSYS / EPERM from seccomp / bwrap detected escape), the system shall mark the session.status='crashed' with crash_reason='sandbox_violation' and append a redline event to audit_logs (action='sandbox.violation').

tickets.title: RLS helper (auth_middleware から PostgreSQL session に auth.uid() を伝播)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The auth_middleware shall set Postgres SET LOCAL request.jwt.claims = '<jwt_claims>' and SET LOCAL request.jwt.claim.sub = '<user_id>' for every authenticated request before any query.
When the request completes (success or error), the system shall RESET the JWT claim variables so the connection can be safely returned to the pool.
While operating without an Authorization header (BUILD_FACTORY_DEV_BYPASS_AUTH=1), the system shall set a deterministic dev user_id for local development, and shall not bypass RLS in production.
Where a request carries custom_permissions JSONB on users, the helper shall expose them to PG via SET LOCAL so RLS policies can read auth.jwt() -> 'custom_permissions'.
If RESET fails or the connection is reused without RESET, the next query shall NOT inherit prior JWT claims (test by issuing a query as anonymous after a logged-in request).

tickets.title: Sentry 設定 (FE+BE / error reporting + perf tracing / graceful degradation)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend shall provide `backend/sentry_config.py` (sentry-sdk graceful import) and the frontend shall provide `frontend/src/lib/sentry.ts` (@sentry/nextjs dynamic import). Both shall expose `init / captureException / captureMessage / setUser / setTag` and shall coexist with structlog (T-S0-11) and audit_logs (memory_service) as 3 independent observability layers.

When init_sentry(dsn, environment, traces_sample_rate, release, send_default_pii) is called, the system shall validate parameters, set the global SDK state (idempotent), and log success/failure to stdlib logging. capture_exception / capture_message shall return event_id or None (no-op when not configured).

While sentry-sdk / @sentry/nextjs is NOT installed OR SENTRY_DSN env var is unset, the system shall fall back gracefully to no-op for all public APIs (no ImportError, no crash). send_default_pii shall default to False (no PII / cookie / IP forwarded). max_breadcrumbs shall be capped at 50.

If invalid traces_sample_rate (outside [0.0, 1.0]) or invalid environment (not in development/staging/production/ci/test) is passed, the system shall raise ValueError. The Sentry module shall NOT write to audit_logs DB (separated concern). The module source SHALL NOT contain hardcoded DSN / API keys (DSN comes from env only).

tickets.title: structlog + pino (backend + frontend 中央集約 logger / 構造化ログ)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend shall provide `backend/logging_config.py` (structlog setup with stdlib logging fallback) and the frontend shall provide `frontend/src/lib/logger.ts` (pino logger). Both shall expose `configure / get_logger / bind_context / clear_context` (backend) and `logger / withContext` (frontend). Existing `import logging` / `logging.getLogger` calls SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE backwards-compat).

When configure_structlog(level, json_output) is called, the system shall set both structlog and stdlib root logger to the requested level. When bind_context(**kwargs) is called, the system shall add the key-value pairs to contextvars so subsequent log emissions auto-merge the context (structlog only; stdlib fallback is no-op).

While structlog is installed, the system shall use JSON output in CI/PROD_LOGGING environments and console output in development; while structlog is NOT installed (minimal CI runtime), the system shall fall back gracefully to stdlib logging without raising ImportError. The logger module shall NOT write to audit_logs DB (audit_logs is a separate concern via memory_service.emit_event).

If structlog/pino import fails, the system shall log a warning via stdlib logging and continue with fallback (no crash). If an invalid log level is passed (not in DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL), the system shall raise ValueError. The logger module shall NOT include secrets / authentication tokens / passwords in log output (caller responsibility; lint check enforced via secrets scanner).

tickets.title: Better Stack uptime monitor (pull /health + push heartbeat / graceful degradation)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend shall provide `backend/uptime_heartbeat.py` exposing `send_heartbeat() / is_configured() / get_heartbeat_url() / APSCHEDULER_INTEGRATION_GUIDE`. The existing `/health` endpoint (backend/main.py L248) shall be reused unmodified for the pull-mode (Better Stack → backend) check. The push-mode (backend → Better Stack heartbeat URL) shall be opt-in via BETTER_STACK_HEARTBEAT_URL env var.

When send_heartbeat() is invoked, the system shall (a) validate URL form (scheme https/http, non-empty netloc), (b) POST or GET to the URL via httpx within `timeout_sec` seconds (default 10), and (c) return True on 2xx/3xx, False on 4xx/5xx/network failure. The function shall NOT raise on network errors (silent degradation, log warning only).

While BETTER_STACK_HEARTBEAT_URL is unset, send_heartbeat() shall return False without any HTTP call (no-op). While set, the module shall NOT modify repository state or audit_logs DB (uptime monitor is a separate observability concern from audit trail). APScheduler integration shall be a guide (docstring), not auto-wired.

If url scheme is not https/http, or netloc is empty, or timeout_sec is outside (0, 60], or extra_payload is not a dict/None, the system shall raise ValueError. If network call fails (timeout / DNS / 5xx), the system shall log a warning and return False (NOT raise). The module source SHALL NOT contain hardcoded heartbeat URLs / API tokens (URL comes from env only).

tickets.title: Supabase project init + 環境変数 (env 必須化、ハードコード鍵除去)

When supabase_client is imported and any required SUPABASE_* env var is missing, the module shall raise a clear configuration error with the missing variable name.

Where .env.example provides example values, those values shall be obvious placeholders (containing 'REPLACE_WITH_' or '<...>') and never look like real Supabase keys.
If a real-looking Supabase key (matching ^sb_(publishable|secret)_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}$) is committed to version control, the lint shall detect and fail.

tickets.title: FastAPI モジュラーモノリス 13 ドメイン bounded-context 整理
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend code shall be organized into 13 bounded-context domain modules under backend/domains/.
Each domain module shall expose only its public interface to other modules via a domains/<name>/__init__.py barrel.
When a domain imports another domain's internal model directly (bypassing the barrel), the linter shall report a violation.
While building the application, the system shall verify the absence of circular dependencies between domain modules.
If circular dependencies are detected between domains, the build shall fail with the cycle path printed.

acceptance_criteria.text: The migration shall create 6 auth tables (users, auth_sessions, user_2fa_secrets, user_2fa_recovery_codes, oauth_connections, auth_audit_log) with explicit FK constraints to auth.users and TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() for created_at columns.
When the migration is applied a second time (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), the system shall not error out and shall be idempotent.
While RLS is enabled on auth_sessions / user_2fa_secrets / user_2fa_recovery_codes / oauth_connections, only the row's owner (auth.uid() = user_id) and service_role shall read or write rows.
Where 2FA is enabled (user_2fa_secrets row exists with enabled=true), the auth_audit_log shall record every TOTP challenge with success=true|false and user_id.

If a duplicate (user_id, provider) is inserted into oauth_connections, the UNIQUE constraint shall reject it; if a 2FA secret is stored without encryption (plaintext), the schema shall not allow it (encrypted_secret column NOT NULL).

tickets.title: 6 ロール enum + permission matrix JSON
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall accept only the 6 roles {owner, ws_admin, contributor, viewer, client, monitor} as workspace_members.role values.
The system shall expose a permission matrix of 30 permission keys mapped across the 6 roles via GET /api/workspaces/permissions/matrix.
When GET /api/workspaces/permissions/matrix is called, the system shall return a JSON object with shape {roles: string[], permissions: {key, label, category}[], matrix: {role: {permission: bool}}}.
If a request supplies a role value outside the 6 allowed roles, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 422 and shall not persist the change.

tickets.title: custom_permissions JSONB バリデータ
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall validate that custom_permissions is a dict[str, bool] whose keys are a subset of the 30 known permission keys.
When a member is added or updated with custom_permissions, the system shall apply per-key overrides on top of the role-default permissions when has_permission() is evaluated.
While custom_permissions overrides exist for a member, the system shall use the override value (true/false) instead of the role-default for that key.
If custom_permissions contains an unknown key or a non-bool value or is not a dict, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 400 and shall not persist the change.

tickets.title: Swarm 並列実行 (Subagent / Task tool + git worktree)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: Swarm shall spawn 4 / 9 / 16 / 64 parallel sessions, each via claude-agent-sdk Subagent (Task tool) + a dedicated git worktree + branch.
When the swarm starts, the system shall allocate one worktree per session under .worktrees/swarm_{pool_id}/cell_{n} to prevent file conflicts.
While a swarm is running, the system shall stream per-cell logs and aggregate stats (queued/running/done/failed/crashed).
Where two cells touch the same file, the system shall serialize them via a file-level lock and shall not allow both to commit simultaneously.
If a Subagent escapes its sandbox or attempts to access another cell's worktree, the system shall kill that cell and emit a redline event.

tickets.title: permission matrix UI grid
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The /workspaces/{id}/members page shall display all members with their role rendered as a 6-option <select> bound to the 6 canonical roles.
The page shall display a representative permission grid (>= 7 columns) sourced from GET /api/workspaces/permissions/matrix, one row per role.
When a user changes a member's role via the select, the system shall send PATCH /api/workspaces/{id}/members/{user_id} with actor_user_id and re-fetch the member list on success.
When a user submits the add-member form, the system shall send POST /api/workspaces/{id}/members and append the new member to the list on success.
If backend returns HTTP 409 with code in {self_strip_blocked, owner_protected}, the system shall display a user-friendly Japanese message instead of the raw error body.

tickets.title: self-strip block + owner 保護
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: If actor_user_id attempts to demote their own role from ws_admin to a lower role, the system shall respond HTTP 409 with detail={code: 'self_strip_blocked'}.
If a delete or role-change request would remove the last remaining owner of a workspace, the system shall respond HTTP 409 with detail={code: 'owner_protected'}.
When the actor changes another member's role (not their own), the system shall apply standard permission checks and shall not raise self_strip or owner_protected.
All 409 responses for this guard shall serialize as {detail: {code, message}} for machine-readable handling on the frontend.

tickets.title: プロフィール編集 UI (existing user_profile 拡張)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The /settings/profile page shall allow editing of display_name, role_text, bio, theme, and avatar_url for the current user.
When the user clicks the save button, the system shall send PATCH /api/bf-profile with the dirty fields and display a success toast on 200.
While the form is dirty (any field changed vs. the loaded value), the system shall enable the save button; otherwise it shall be disabled.
If the backend returns a 5xx, the system shall preserve the user's input and display an error toast indicating the save failed.

tickets.title: API キー管理 UI (existing credentials_store 活用)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The /settings/profile API-keys tab shall display the connection status of Slack, GitHub, and Anthropic OAuth providers.
When the user clicks the connect button for a provider, the system shall request GET /api/oauth/{provider}/authorize, store the returned state in sessionStorage, and navigate to authorize_url.
When the user clicks the disconnect button, the system shall send DELETE /api/oauth/{provider} and update the displayed status to 'unconnected' on success.
If the OAuth callback state does not match the value in sessionStorage, the system shall clear sessionStorage and display a CSRF error toast.

tickets.title: pgsodium 暗号化保管
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: Every value persisted to encrypted_credentials.ciphertext shall be encrypted with pgsodium (or fernet-compatible fallback) and shall never be stored as plain text.
When get_credential(user_id, provider) is called, the system shall decrypt the stored ciphertext with the active key and return the plain text to the caller.
While multiple key versions exist (after rotation), the system shall use key_version to select the correct key for decryption of each row.
If no decryption key is configured at runtime, the system shall raise RuntimeError on get_credential and shall not silently return None or the ciphertext.

tickets.title: OAuth 連携 (Slack/GitHub/Anthropic)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall support OAuth integration for providers in {slack, github, anthropic} with authorize / callback / disconnect / status endpoints.
When the OAuth provider redirects back with a valid code, the system shall exchange it for an access_token and persist it via the credentials_store (encrypted).
When the provider returns an error in the callback, the system shall not persist any credential and shall display a user-friendly error to the frontend.
If the state parameter on the callback does not match the value issued by /authorize, the system shall reject the callback with HTTP 400 (csrf_mismatch).

tickets.title: クローン opt-in toggle + GDPR 削除権 (30 日 grace)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The user_lifecycle table shall persist clone_optin (bool) and deletion_requested_at (nullable timestamp) per user.
When a user submits a deletion request, the system shall set deletion_requested_at = now() and shall NOT hard-delete the user until 30 days later (grace period).
When a user cancels a pending deletion within the grace window, the system shall set deletion_requested_at = NULL and restore the account to active state.
If a user attempts to submit a deletion request while one is already pending, the system shall respond HTTP 409 with detail={code: 'already_pending'}.

tickets.title: LiteLLM docker-compose 追加 (ADR-010 Layer 2b サブ用途: cheap-batch / image / voice / fallback)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall add a `litellm` service to `docker-compose.yml` (port 4000, image `ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:main-stable`) and provide `monitoring/litellm-config.yaml` defining ADR-010 Layer 2b routes (cheap-batch=Gemini Flash / image-gen=Gemini Image+DALL-E / whisper / anthropic-fallback=GPT-4o / gemini-fallback). The service shall be opt-in via `profiles: [litellm]` (Phase 1 manual start). Main path (claude-agent-sdk + anthropic-python) shall NOT route through LiteLLM.

When `docker compose --profile litellm up -d litellm` is run, the litellm service shall start on port 4000 with healthcheck `/health/liveliness`, authenticated via LITELLM_MASTER_KEY env. The config shall include `num_retries: 2` and `request_timeout: 60` for graceful failure handling (cooperates with T-AI-06 tenacity).

While the litellm service runs, the system shall mount `monitoring/litellm-config.yaml` read-only and shall persist API keys only via env vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY). The service shall use json_logs:true (T-S0-11 structlog 整合) and shall redact user API key info in logs. PII / secrets shall NOT appear in service logs.

If LITELLM_MASTER_KEY env is missing, the service shall fail to start (no anonymous access). If hardcoded API keys appear in docker-compose.yml or litellm-config.yaml (literal sk-* / eyJ* / google-api JWT), the lint script shall fail. The service shall NOT be started by default (profiles requirement), preventing accidental usage in main path.

tickets.title: Memory 3 tier (claude-agent-sdk session + Memory API + Mem0 + Obsidian)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The memory service shall expose a unified API combining: Tier 1 (claude-agent-sdk session, raw messages), Tier 2 (compressed + 9-section summary auto-generated by SDK), Tier 3 (Anthropic Memory API + Mem0 vector + Obsidian markdown).
When the SDK's auto compaction triggers at 95% context, the system shall persist the 9-section summary to chat_messages and emit a memory_compacted event to audit_logs.
When a new session starts that references a prior session_id, the system shall merge: SDK session_resume + Memory API recall + Mem0 vector top-5 + Constitution into the system prompt.
While Anthropic Memory API is available, the system shall write durable facts there as primary; Mem0 holds the vector copy for similarity search.
Where the user opts in to Obsidian sync, the system shall mirror Tier 3 facts to ~/Documents/会社運営DB/obsidian/ as Markdown files.
If Memory API fails, the system shall fall back to Mem0 only and emit a memory_degraded event; it shall not silently drop facts.

tickets.title: 組織図 UI (React Flow tree / AI 社員 hierarchy 可視化 / Lucide + eb-500)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The frontend shall provide `frontend/src/components/org/OrganizationChart.tsx` exposing a `<OrganizationChart employees={...} edges={...} onNodeClick={...} />` component that renders AI employees as a React Flow tree, with role_level (secretary/leader/member) determining vertical row position. The component shall reuse existing `@xyflow/react` (already in package.json) and shall NOT modify `DependencyGraph.tsx` (REUSE).

When the user clicks a node, the component shall invoke `onNodeClick(employee)` with the original EmployeeData object. Layout shall recompute via `useMemo` when `employees` or `edges` props change. The component shall be controlled (no internal employees state).

While the component renders, all colors shall use ENGINE BASE green palette (`border-eb-500` / `border-eb-400` / `border-eb-200` / `bg-eb-50`) per CLAUDE.md §5.2, and all icons shall be Lucide (Crown for secretary, Users for leader, User for member) — NO emojis (CLAUDE.md §5.1). The component shall NOT mutate input props or write to any backend store.

If `employees` is empty, null, or contains only inactive/invalid records, the component shall render a fallback message (`org-chart-empty` test-id) instead of crashing. If `role_level` is not in [secretary, leader, member], the node shall be excluded silently. The component SHALL NOT contain hardcoded color literals outside the eb-* palette.

tickets.title: BMAD 12 → 10 メンバー persona prompt 整理 (md source-of-truth + loader)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `data/personas/bmad/{persona_key}.md` for all 10 Build-Factory personas (mary/preston/winston/sally/devon/quinn/reviewer/brand/mockup/logan) following a 7-section format (Role/Personality/Tone Style/Catchphrase/Specialty/Constraints/Handoff). The 12→10 BMAD mapping shall be documented in `data/personas/bmad/README.md`. A loader `backend/services/bmad_persona_prompts.py` exposes `load_persona_prompt(persona_key)` and `list_personas()`. Existing `ai_employee_store` / `delegation_service` / `secretary_chat` SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE).

When `load_persona_prompt(persona_key)` is called, the system shall return the md file content within 100ms (1ms with cache hit) or None if file missing. `get_prompt_validation_status(persona_key)` shall return dict {persona_key, available, missing_sections, char_count} verifying 7 required sections are present.

While the loader is in use, the DB seed (`ai_personas` table from supabase migration 20260512400000) remains the cache layer; the md files are the source of truth. The module SHALL NOT write to audit_logs DB (read-only loader). Path traversal SHALL be prevented (resolve().relative_to base dir check).

If `persona_key` is not in VALID_PERSONA_KEYS (10 keys), empty, non-string, or contains characters outside `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`, the system shall raise ValueError (caller bug). If a md file is missing or read fails, `load_persona_prompt` shall log a warning and return None (graceful, no raise). The module SHALL NOT contain hardcoded secrets and SHALL NOT make external HTTP calls.

tickets.title: AI 社員召喚 API + Workspace Dashboard (existing secretary_chat/delegation_service 統合, claude-agent-sdk handoff)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The dashboard shall display 5 KPI cards (progress, completed tasks, running sessions, monthly cost, pending approvals).
When the user opens the dashboard, the system shall load aggregated stats within 800ms (P95).
While sessions are running, the system shall show pulse-dot animation on the running count.
While the backend handles handoff between AI personas, it shall use claude-agent-sdk Subagent (Task tool) and shall NOT introduce LangGraph / LangChain in the main runner path (ADR-010).
Where the user has access to multiple workspaces, the system shall provide quick switch via the sidebar.
If the user tries to access a workspace without permission, the system shall return 403 and shall not render the dashboard.
If LangGraph or LangChain imports are introduced in the handoff path of secretary_chat / delegation_service, the lint script shall fail (ADR-010 §UNWANTED).

tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The Intent Router entry node shall use claude-agent-sdk (Anthropic official) as the runtime layer and shall NOT introduce LangGraph. The entry node receives a user message + session_id and returns the chosen persona (mary/devon/quinn/...) for handoff to T-M27-03.

When the entry node receives a request, it shall classify intent (via T-M27-02 wrapper) and emit audit_logs event_type='m27.entry_node.dispatched' with chosen_persona, latency_ms, and session_id within 2 seconds.
While the entry node is active, the system shall apply Row Level Security on audit_logs and shall persist routing decisions to sessions table per CLAUDE.md §5.3.

If LangGraph/LangChain imports are introduced in this module, the lint script shall fail (ADR-010). If invalid input or unauthorized actor is detected, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall not mutate persistent state.

tickets.title: Intent 分類 (existing intent_preprocessor/mode_detector/skill_detector 統合)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The intent_classifier service shall provide a unified read API (classify()) that runs the 3 existing detectors (intent_preprocessor.detect_explicit_intent / mode_detector.detect_mode / skill_detector.detect_skill) in parallel and returns a normalized IntentSignal dict with top_signal priority (explicit > skill > mode). The existing 3 detector modules shall NOT be modified (REFACTOR backwards-compat).

When classify() is invoked, the system shall execute the 3 detectors in parallel via asyncio, apply the top_signal priority rule, and emit an audit_logs entry with event_type='intent.classified' carrying top_signal_kind/value/mode/skill/has_explicit/latency_ms within 2 seconds.

While the classifier is active, the system shall preserve the existing 3 detector module symbol surfaces (cross-module assert) and shall maintain backwards compatibility (REFACTOR coverage >= baseline). Read endpoints (health) shall NOT emit audit events.

If the SDK / alternative LLM classifier backend (register_classifier_backend) returns invalid output (missing keys / invalid mode / wrong types), the system shall fall back to the 3-detector default path with a warning log (silent failure 防止). If invalid input or unauthorized actor is detected, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall NOT mutate persistent state. If application code re-implements intent classification in a way that bypasses the 3 detectors (e.g., inlined keyword matching outside the existing modules), the lint script shall flag it during code review.

tickets.title: Agent / Role Selector + handoff (SDK Task tool activation + ai_employees lookup wrapper)
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall use claude-agent-sdk's built-in Subagent / Task tool for AI employee handoff (mary -> devon -> quinn etc.) and shall NOT re-implement a handoff/orchestration layer. Role selection looks up the target persona from existing ai_employees CRUD (T-022-03).
When a handoff is invoked, the system shall record an audit_logs entry with event_type='m27.handoff', source_persona, target_persona, session_id, and timestamp within 2 seconds.
While a handoff is in flight, the system shall maintain the SDK session context (claude-agent-sdk session resume) and shall apply Row Level Security on audit_logs / sessions per CLAUDE.md §5.3.

If application code re-implements handoff logic (manual message routing between personas, custom Subagent dispatcher) instead of using SDK Task tool, the lint script shall fail (ADR-010). If invalid persona / unauthorized actor / unknown session is detected, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall not mutate persistent state.

tickets.title: Tier 1 tool result trimming (SDK auto activation + audit wrapper)
tickets.label: REUSE

When the SDK trims a tool result during a session, the system shall record an audit_logs entry with event_type='tier1.tool_result_trimmed', the session_id, original size, and trimmed size.

While the SDK trim feature is active, the system shall preserve the original tool result in chat_messages (no destructive mutation) and shall apply Row Level Security on audit_logs per CLAUDE.md §5.3.

If application code re-implements tool result trimming logic (size cap / age cap / dedup), the lint script shall fail. If invalid input or unauthorized actor is detected, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall not mutate persistent state.

tickets.title: Tier 2 prompt cache friendly (cache_control: ephemeral 5min) — SDK + anthropic-python auto activation
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall enable cache_control={'type':'ephemeral'} on all anthropic-python and claude-agent-sdk calls (TTL 5min) and shall NOT re-implement prompt caching logic in application code (ADR-010 / requirements §11.6 STATE).
When an anthropic-python response includes usage.cache_read_input_tokens or usage.cache_creation_input_tokens, the system shall record both values in cost_logs along with session_id and timestamp.

While prompt cache is active, the system shall expose a daily cache_hit_rate metric (cache_read / (cache_read + cache_creation + input)) and shall apply Row Level Security on cost_logs per CLAUDE.md §5.3.

If application code re-implements prompt caching or attempts to manage TTL manually, the lint script shall fail. If invalid input or unauthorized actor is detected, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall not mutate persistent state.

tickets.title: Tier 3 9-section structured summary persistence (SDK auto-generated, DB persist wrapper only)

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall persist the 9-section structured summary auto-generated by claude-agent-sdk at 95% context into chat_messages.compressed_summary, and shall NOT re-implement the summarization logic (no keyword heuristic, no LLM call from application code — ADR-010 / requirements §11.6 EVENT).

When the SDK emits a memory_compacted event (95% threshold reached), the system shall persist the 9 sections to chat_messages.compressed_summary and emit audit_logs event_type='memory_compacted' with summary_message_id and section keys within 2 seconds.
While persistence is in progress, the system shall preserve original messages in chat_messages (no destructive mutation) and shall apply Row Level Security + audit_logs per CLAUDE.md §5.3.

If application code generates the 9-section summary itself (keyword heuristic, LLM call from application layer, or any rule-based extractor), the lint script shall fail. If the SDK output schema is invalid (missing any of 9 sections), the system shall reject persistence with a 4xx response and shall not mutate persistent state.

tickets.title: Memory 統合 orchestrator (memory_pipeline; 3 層 short+mid+long → context 組立) + 統合テスト
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The memory_pipeline module shall integrate the 3-tier memory layers (short=raw chat_messages via chat_thread_store / mid=9-section compressed_summary via mid_term_layer / long=Mem0+Obsidian via long_term_layer) and assemble a unified context block for AI session prompts. Integration tests shall verify cross-layer invariants (9-section SECTION_KEYS / tier ordering / source attribution). The module shall NOT modify the underlying 3 layer modules (NEW + REUSE).

When build_context(thread_id, query) is invoked, the system shall fetch all 3 tiers in parallel via asyncio and return an assembled context dict within 2 seconds, emitting audit_logs entry 'memory.context_built' with tier_hit_count and total_chars.

While the orchestrator is active, the system shall NOT modify any of the underlying layer modules (chat_thread_store / mid_term_layer / long_term_layer) and shall preserve their existing API contracts. If any single tier fails to fetch, the system shall degrade gracefully (return partial context + log warning) rather than abort the whole pipeline.

If a tier fetch raises an exception, the system shall log the warning and continue with remaining tiers (graceful degradation). If invalid input (empty thread_id / oversized query / non-positive max_tokens) is provided, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall NOT mutate persistent state. If application code re-implements 3-tier compaction in the orchestrator instead of delegating to the layer modules, the lint script shall flag it during code review (ADR-010 cross-ref with T-M28-02/03/04 UNWANTED).

tickets.title: Sprint 2 統合テスト (M-27 chain x M-30 chain x 4 層 observability x Layer 2b cross-feature)

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `backend/tests/test_t_it_s2_sprint2_integration.py` verifying cross-feature integration of Sprint 2 deliverables: (a) M-27 chain (intent_classifier → handoff_service), (b) M-30 chain (mid_term_layer ↔ memory_pipeline), (c) 4-layer observability coexistence (logging_config + sentry_config + uptime_heartbeat without conflicts), (d) Layer 2b LiteLLM config integrity. Existing unit tests SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE).

When the integration test runs, it shall execute scenarios that span 2+ modules per assertion, simulating real call sequences (classify → handoff / record_summary → latest_summary / configure_structlog while sentry init in same process). Each scenario shall complete within 2 seconds.

While the integration test runs, the system shall NOT make real network calls (all external IO must be mocked via monkeypatch), shall NOT write to the audit_logs DB (capture via fake_emit), and shall preserve module state via pytest fixtures (reset_for_tests / clear_context after each test).

If any Sprint 2 module's public API breaks (function signature change / removed symbol), the integration test shall fail with a clear error message identifying the broken contract. The test SHALL NOT depend on external services (LiteLLM container / Sentry SaaS / Better Stack) and SHALL NOT contain hardcoded secrets.

tickets.title: テンプレート構造を確定 (templates/project-bootstrap/)
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: The repository shall contain templates/project-bootstrap/ with all required skeleton files (CLAUDE.md.j2, HANDOVER.md.j2, IMPLEMENTATION_PROTOCOL.md, lint-mock.sh, validate-tickets.py, settings.json).
The templates/CHANGELOG.md shall record every change to the template with version bump.
When the template structure is updated, the system shall update templates/CHANGELOG.md before merging.
If a required skeleton file is missing from templates/project-bootstrap/, the lint script shall fail.

tickets.title: WorkspaceService.bootstrap() — 新案件作成時にテンプレを GitHub repo に展開
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: When a new workspace is created via POST /api/workspaces, the system shall automatically create a GitHub repo (engine-base/proj-{slug}) and populate it with templates/project-bootstrap/ files.
When the bootstrap completes, the system shall make an initial commit with message 'chore: bootstrap project from template v{X}' on the main branch.
While the bootstrap is in progress, the system shall set workspace.status='bootstrapping' and refuse other operations until status becomes 'ready'.
Where the user provided a custom GitHub org, the system shall create the repo under that org instead of the default.
If the GitHub API call fails, the system shall mark workspace.status='bootstrap_failed', preserve the workspace row for retry, and shall not leave a partial repo on GitHub.

tickets.title: Jinja2 プレースホルダ置換エンジン
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall render every .j2 file in templates/project-bootstrap/ using Jinja2 with the workspace metadata as context.
The system shall support 10 placeholders: project_name, project_slug, client_name, deadline, phase, owner_email, tech_stack, ai_employees, template_version, generated_at.
When a required placeholder is missing from the workspace metadata, the system shall raise BootstrapError before writing any file (atomic).
While rendering, the system shall apply autoescape=False (Markdown / HTML output preserved).
If a rendered file still contains {{ }} (unrendered placeholder), the system shall fail validation and shall not commit.

tickets.title: 既存案件への遡及適用 (build-factory project migrate)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CLI command `build-factory project migrate --workspace={id}` shall fetch the workspace's repo, diff with the latest template, and add only missing files.
When an existing file would be overwritten, the system shall skip it and report a manual-merge needed.
While dry-run mode is on (--dry-run), the system shall print the diff but shall not commit or push.
Where --all is specified, the system shall iterate through every workspace and migrate sequentially.
If the workspace repo is dirty (uncommitted changes), the system shall abort the migration and shall not force any change.

tickets.title: テンプレ更新時に全案件へ PR 自動作成 (CI 統合)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When templates/CHANGELOG.md is updated on main, the GitHub Action shall trigger a dry-run migrate against every active workspace.
The system shall report the total number of files that would change per workspace, awaiting masato approval before proceeding.
When masato approves via the workflow_dispatch input, the system shall create a PR titled 'chore: migrate to template v{X}' on each workspace repo.
While a workspace's last migration is pending review, the system shall not create a duplicate PR for the same template version.
If any workspace migration fails (auth / network), the system shall continue with the others and report failures in the summary, not abort the whole job.

tickets.title: e2e テスト = workspace 作成 → 強制レイヤー検証
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When the e2e test creates a new workspace, the resulting GitHub repo shall contain CLAUDE.md, docs/HANDOVER.md, scripts/lint-mock.sh, scripts/validate-tickets.py, and .claude/settings.json.
The test shall verify that grep -c 'Build-Factory' CLAUDE.md returns >= 1 in the bootstrapped repo.
When the test runs `bash scripts/lint-mock.sh` on the bootstrapped repo, it shall pass (no emojis, no AGPL, no missing files).
While running CI, this test shall execute under 60 seconds.
If any required file is missing from the bootstrapped repo, the test shall fail with a clear error indicating the missing path.

tickets.title: LiteLLM Router (サブ用途のみ — 画像/音声/安価バッチ/緊急代替)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: LiteLLM shall be used ONLY for sub-purpose routes (image generation, speech, batch with cheaper providers, emergency Anthropic-outage fallback) — NOT for the main Claude Code implementation engine.
When an image-gen request arrives, the system shall route to Gemini Image / DALL-E via LiteLLM.
When Anthropic API is unhealthy (3 consecutive 5xx within 60s), the system shall switch the main route to LiteLLM → GPT-4o or Gemini 2.5 Pro for the duration of the outage.
While in fallback mode, the system shall mark every response with provider=fallback and disable Memory API writes (which only Anthropic supports).
If a developer attempts to route the main Claude Code engine through LiteLLM (e.g., importing LiteLLM from claude-runner code), the lint script shall fail.

tickets.title: Anthropic Memory API 統合 (永続記憶の主たる保存先)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall integrate Anthropic Memory API as the primary store for cross-session durable facts (decisions, preferences, context).
When a session ends with status=completed, the system shall extract durable facts (D-XXX style) and write them to Memory API scoped by user/workspace.
When a new session starts, the system shall recall relevant Memory API entries and inject them into the system prompt within 200ms.
While storing facts, the system shall tag each entry with source_session_id and confidence_score (0.0–1.0).
Where the user requests memory deletion, the system shall remove the fact from Memory API + Mem0 + Obsidian within 24 hours and emit an audit event.
If Memory API write fails, the system shall queue the fact for retry and shall not lose the data.

tickets.title: Mem0 ベクトル検索 + Anthropic Memory API ブリッジ
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The bridge shall mirror every Memory API write to Mem0 (pgvector) for similarity search.
When a session asks 'recall similar past decisions to X', the system shall query Mem0 vector top-5 within 300ms and re-rank against Memory API results.
While the user is the secretary AI, the bridge shall preload top-50 most-relevant facts into the system prompt at session start.
If Mem0 and Memory API diverge (e.g., a fact exists in one but not the other), the bridge shall surface the discrepancy in audit_logs and shall not silently pick one.

tickets.title: chat_messages 全文検索 (pg_trgm + pgvector ハイブリッド)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall expose a hybrid search API combining pg_trgm (lexical similarity) and pgvector (semantic similarity) over chat_messages.content.
When a user types a query in S-011 global search, the system shall return ranked results (hybrid score = 0.5*trgm + 0.5*vector) within 500ms (P95).
Where the user adds 'date:2026-04', the system shall narrow results to that date range.
While indexing, the system shall use pg_partman to partition chat_messages by month for fast archival.
If a query attempts to search across workspaces the user has no access to, RLS shall block it and the result count shall be 0.

tickets.title: Constitution 自動注入エンジン (全 AI 社員のシステムプロンプト)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: Every AI employee session shall have the latest Constitution version (masato's judgment criteria) injected into the system prompt as a stable cached prefix.
When the Constitution is updated (new version saved in S-018), the system shall invalidate prompt cache for all active sessions on next turn.
While a session is operating as 'secretary' AI, the system shall additionally inject the full constitution body (all sections); for other roles, only Sections 2 (values) + 4 (red lines).
Where a workspace overrides specific constitution sections (workspace-level Constitution), the system shall merge with masato's base constitution, workspace winning on conflict.
If the Constitution file is corrupted or missing, the system shall block all AI sessions and alert masato — it shall not run AI without constitution.

tickets.title: Cost tracking (Anthropic Usage API + LiteLLM callback で 案件/AI/日次集計)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: Every Claude API call (via claude-agent-sdk) and every LiteLLM call shall record one cost_logs row containing: provider, model, in/out tokens, cache_read/write tokens, USD cost, session_id, workspace_id, ai_employee_id.
When a session completes, the system shall reconcile its cumulative cost with Anthropic Usage API (daily) and flag discrepancies > 5%.
While monthly cost on a workspace exceeds the configured budget, the system shall pause that workspace's queue and notify masato.
While prompt cache is hit, cost_logs.cache_read_tokens shall be > 0 and the displayed cost shall reflect the 90% discount on cached input.
If cost recording fails, the session shall continue but emit a cost_recording_failed audit event for manual reconciliation.

tickets.title: Rate limit 自動 retry (tenacity 指数バックオフ 2/4/8/16s)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When Anthropic API returns 429 (rate limit) or 529 (overloaded), the system shall retry with exponential backoff: 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, then fail.
When a transient 5xx is returned, the system shall retry up to 3 times before failing.
While retrying, the system shall preserve the session_id and idempotency-key so the same logical request is not duplicated.
If a non-retryable error (4xx other than 429) is returned, the system shall NOT retry and shall surface the error immediately.

tickets.title: Streaming UI (claude-agent-sdk → WebSocket bridge)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When claude-agent-sdk emits a streaming token, the system shall forward it via WebSocket to the subscribed frontend within 50ms.
When a tool_use event occurs, the system shall send a structured ws message {type:'tool_use', tool, input} and append to session_logs.
While the WebSocket disconnects, the system shall buffer messages for 30s and replay on reconnect.
Where the client requests log replay, the system shall replay session_logs since a given seq_id.
If the buffer overflows (> 1MB per session), the system shall drop the oldest streaming messages and emit a buffer_overflow event but shall preserve tool_use and final result.

tickets.title: Anthropic 障害時 LiteLLM フォールバック (Claude → GPT-4o / Gemini)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When Anthropic API health-check fails 3 times within 60 seconds, the system shall switch the main route to LiteLLM → GPT-4o (primary fallback) and notify masato.
While in fallback mode, the system shall disable Memory API writes and Subagent (Task tool), and shall mark every session with degraded_mode=true.
When Anthropic recovers (3 consecutive successful health-checks in 5 min), the system shall switch back to claude-agent-sdk automatically.
Where masato manually overrides via /admin/fallback {provider:gemini}, the system shall route to Gemini 2.5 Pro instead of GPT-4o.

If both Anthropic AND OpenAI fail simultaneously, the system shall pause new sessions, allow only emergency read-only access, and alert masato + Slack — it shall not silently route to an untested provider.
